crisis: Cyclone Senyar and floods in Indonesia

tavily.answer: Recent floods and landslides in Indonesia, particularly in Sumatra, have had devastating effects on the critically endangered Tapanuli orangutan population. Studies indicate that at least 7% of the world's rarest great apes were killed, with 58 out of the approximately 800 Tapanuli orangutans lost. The extreme rainfall and landslides, exacerbated by Cyclone Senyar, have not only claimed human lives and destroyed homes but also posed a significant threat to the survival of these endangered species. The rapid deforestation of Sumatra further worsened the impact, highlighting the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect both human communities and wildlife.
